next_steps: Immediate transfer to the emergency department for stabilization
Obtain 12-lead electrocardiogram (ECG) to differentiate tachycardia rhythm
Interrogate implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) to evaluate for recorded tachycardia and history of antitachycardia pacing
Obtain serum electrolytes, thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), troponin, and repeat NT-proBNP
Perform bedside transthoracic echocardiography to assess hemodynamic function and prosthetic valve integrity
Administer intravenous fluids if hemodynamically unstable, pending evaluation of volume status

differential_diagnoses: Atrial flutter with rapid ventricular response
next_steps: Immediate transfer to the emergency department for cardiac stabilization
12-lead electrocardiogram to evaluate for narrow vs. wide complex tachycardia
Cardiac telemetry monitoring and serial blood pressure assessment
Laboratory evaluation: Troponin, electrolytes (K, Mg), CBC, lactate, and renal function
Bedside focused cardiac ultrasound (POCUS) to assess volume status and cardiac output
Urgent medication reconciliation to screen for pro-arrhythmic interactions
